---
title: "The ggsql knitr engine"
knitr:
  opts_chunk:
    collapse: true
    comment: "#>"
description: |
  An introduction to using the ggsql knitr engine in Rmarkdown and 
  Quarto
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{The ggsql knitr engine}
  %\VignetteEngine{quarto::html}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

```{r setup}
library(ggsql)
```

The main selling point of the ggsql R package is arguably it's knitr engine that allows you to add `{ggsql}` blocks to your Rmarkdown and Quarto documents. While ggsql also provides a Jupyter kernel that Quarto can use, Jupyter only allows a single kernel at a time, so if you wish to mix R and/or Python blocks with ggsql visualizations, then the ggsql knitr engine is the way.
